---
title: "Microclimate.jl"
subtitle: "A new package for nicroclimate modelling in the BiophysicalEcology ecosystem"
author: 
  name: "Rafael Schouten, Michael Kearney"
  affiliation: "University of Melbourne"
---

---

## Microclimate.jl

A Julia rewrite of the core physics of [NicheMapR](https://github.com/mrke/NicheMapR) (Kearney & Porter 2017).

A core component of our new BiophysicalEcology github group

![](figures/be.png){.nostretch fig-align="center" width="70%"}

# Microclimate modelling


::: {.incremental}
- Microclimate modellers are greeedy
- We want *small-scale* microclimate predictions over *large* areas, and often high temporal resolution.
- This means heavy computations
- But we also need to quickly explore new ideas
:::

::: {.fragment}

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
*We need levers to easily switch between research modes*

::: {.incremental}
- spatial resolution
- temporal resolution
- formulation accuracy
- formulation completeness
:::
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](figures/lever.png)
:::

:::

:::

# The BiophyisicalEcology approach

::: {.incremental}
- {{< iconify ph:list-checks size=1.4em >}} &nbsp;**Declarative**: scripts describe *what* to solve, not *how*
- {{< iconify mdi:toy-brick-outline size=1.4em >}} &nbsp;**Modular**: data sources and model component can be swapped for alternatives
- {{< iconify ph:ruler size=1.4em >}} &nbsp;**Unit-safe**: temperatures, pressures, lengths, fluxes carry units that are checked.
- {{< iconify ph:book-open-text size=1.4em >}} &nbsp;**Readable**: formulations are short and look like the math in the paper
- {{< iconify ph:lightning size=1.4em >}} &nbsp;**Fast**: must be as fast or faster than existing packages
- {{< iconify simple-icons:julia size=1.4em >}} &nbsp;**Language**: Julia
:::
---

![](figures/clbg_summary-zoomed-out.jpg){fig-align="center"}

---

## Modularity (and units) example

::: {.vcenter}
`FluidProperties.jl` provides `vapour_pressure` formulations. 
{{< iconify tabler:lever size=1.4em >}}

```julia
using FluidProperties, Unitful

# Three formulas, same interface
vp = vapour_pressure(GoffGratch(), 273.15u"K")
vp = vapour_pressure(Teten(), 0.0u"°C") 
vp = vapour_pressure(Huang(), 0.0u"°C")       

# Or we can use a lookup table
vplookup = VapourPressureLookup(GoffGratch()) # 🕹️
vp = vapour_pressure(vplookup, 273.15u"K")
```

:::

---

::: {.vcenter}
::: {.fragment}
### Terminology

**Model**: An abstract specification of a physical model without context

**Problem**: A model with a specific place and time that we can `solve` it for
:::
:::

---

## Solve a single microclimate

A `MicroModel` declares what physics to run: soil, hydraulics, snow.

```julia
using Microclimate, Unitful
depths  = [0.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0, 30.0, 50.0, 100.0, 200.0]u"cm"
heights = [0.01, 2.0]u"m"
micro_model = MicroModel(;
    depths, heights,
    soil_properties_model = CampbelldeVriesSoilProperties(;
        de_vries_shape_factor = 0.1,            # 0.33 organic, 0.1 mineral
        mineral_conductivity  = 1.25u"W/m/K",
        mineral_heat_capacity = 870.0u"J/kg/K",
        recirculation_power   = 4.0,
        return_flow_threshold = 0.162,          # m³/m³
    ),
    soil_hydraulic_model = CampbellSoilHydraulics(;   # plant-side scalars
        root_resistance              = 2.5e10u"m^3/kg/s",
        stomatal_closure_potential   = -1500.0u"J/kg",
        leaf_resistance              = 2.0e6u"m^4/kg/s",
        stomatal_stability_parameter = 10.0,
        root_radius                  = 0.001u"m",
    ),
    snow_model = SnowModel(), # 🕹️ NoSnow() if you dont need it
)
```

---

::: {.vcenter}
Combined with forcings we can define a `MicroProblem`

```julia
inputs = MicroInputs(;
    site = example_site(),
    soil_profile = SoilProfile(;
        bulk_density = fill(2.56u"Mg/m^3", length(depths)),
        mineral_density = fill(2.56u"Mg/m^3", length(depths)),
        hydraulics = example_campbell_hydraulic_profile(depths),
    ),
    environment_minmax = example_monthly_weather(),
    environment_daily  = example_daily_environment(),
    environment_hourly = example_hourly_environment(),
    initial_soil_moisture = fill(0.105, length(depths)),
)
problem = MicroProblem(micro_model, inputs)
output = solve(problem)
```
:::                                         

---

::: {.vcenter}
We cheated using `example_inputs`, but that was still a lot of code! 

We dont want to do that repeatedly, we want to declare our datasources...
:::                                         
 
---

## Declarative data sources: MicroclimateMapper.jl

::: {.vcenter}
A `MicroMapModel` declares what data to use with a model using
`RasterDataSources.jl` data sources.

```julia
using RasterDataSources # For SRTM, NCEP and others

map_model = MicroMapModel(micro_model;   # Reuse the micro model
    dem_source = SRTM,                   # Download SRTM elevation tiles
    weather_source = NCEP{SurfaceGauss}, # Use NCEP daily+hourly 🕹️
    surface_albedo_source = 0.15,        # We declare constants too
    roughness_height_source = 0.04u"m",  # Must have correct units!
)
```
:::                                         

---

::: {.vcenter}
A problem pairs it with spatial points, the year range, and initial conditions.

```julia
points = [
    (3.520, 44.143),   # Camprieu (valley, ~900 m)
    (3.582, 44.115),   # South-facing slope (~1200 m)
    (3.581, 44.122),   # Mont Aigoual summit (~1567 m)
]

problem = MicroVectorProblem(map_model;
    points,
    years = 2010:2010,
    soil_profile = example_soil_profile(depths),
    init = (;
        soil_moisture = fill(0.25, length(depths)),
        snow_depth = 0.0u"cm",
    ),
)

output = solve(problem) # 63 seconds, 365 days 24 hours, laptop
```
:::                                         

---

## Winter snowpack — Mont Aigoual, 2010

![](figures/point_snow_depth.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

---

## A representative winter day

![](figures/point_temperatures.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

---

## Gridded outputs: `MicroRasterProblem`

Like `MicroVectorProblem`, but with a raster `template` instead of fixed points.

```julia
area = Extent(X = (3.554, 3.608), Y = (44.095, 44.149))   # Mont Aigoual
years = 2000:2000 # one year
template = SRTM # run on the SRTM grid
soil_profile = example_soil_profile(depths),

map_model = MicroMapModel(micro_model;         # Same micro model
    dem_source = SRTM,                         # SRTM elevation 
    weather_source = TerraClimate{Historical}, # Terraclimate monthly+hourly 🕹️
    surface_albedo_source = 0.15,              # Contants
    roughness_height_source = 0.04u"m",       # ...
)

problem = MicroRasterProblem(map_model; area, years, template, soil_profile)
output = solve(problem) # 80 seconds, 66*66, monthly 24 hours, 1.25 million solves. Laptop
```

---

## Mont Aigoual, Cévennes — July 2000

![](figures/grid_soil_temperature_5cm_drape.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

Shaded valleys take longer to warm up, east facing slopes are cooler in the afternoon.

---

## Swap the weather source, change resolution — NCEP daily

::: {.vcenter}
```julia
# Lets use SRTM as a template, but larger area, lower resolution
area = Extent(X=(3.33, 3.83), Y=(43.87, 44.37))
srtm = Raster(SRTM; extent=area, lazy=true)
template = aggregate(mean, crop(srtm; to=area, touches=true), 6)  # 🕹️

map_model = MicroMapModel(micro_model;
    dem_source = SRTM,
    weather_source = NCEP{SurfaceGauss}, # NCEP daily+hourly 🕹️
    surface_albedo_source = 0.15,
    roughness_height_source = 0.04u"m",
)
problem = MicroRasterProblem(map_model; area, years, soil_profile, template)
output = solve(problem) # 27 minutes: 100x100, 24 hours 365 days - 87 million solves. Laptop. 
```
:::

---

![](figures/grid_snow_depth_yearly.gif){fig-align="center"}

---

## Status & roadmap

::: {.vcenter}
::: {.incremental}
- Microclimate.jl is validated against NicheMapR Fortran
- BiophysicalEcology github organization is active with 20 repositories
- Packages not yet registered, but soon!
- Cold-air drainage and water runoff models on the way in MicroclimateMapper.jl
- Users and contributions welcome!
:::
:::

---

## Thanks

::: {.incremental}
- **Mike Kearney** — NicheMapR, biophysical ecology vision
- Open source foundations: JuliaGeo (spatial data), Makie (plots), SciML (solvers)
- github.com/orgs/BiophysicalEcology/
:::

::: {.fragment}

```{dot}
//| fig-width: 12
//| fig-height: 5.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent";
  rankdir=LR;
  splines=curved;
  nodesep=0.18;
  ranksep=0.7;
  pad=0.15;
  fontname="Palatino,Charter,Georgia,serif";

  node [
    shape=box,
    style="rounded,filled",
    fillcolor="#fffff8",
    color="#666666",
    fontname="Palatino,Charter,Georgia,serif",
    fontsize=12,
    fontcolor="#111111",
    margin="0.18,0.08",
    penwidth=0.9
  ];

  edge [
    color="#999999",
    penwidth=0.9,
    arrowsize=0.6,
    arrowhead=vee
  ];

  subgraph cluster_base {
    label=<<i>Base</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    UF  [label="Unitful.jl"];
    SOL [label="SolarRadiation.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    FLU [label="FluidProperties.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  subgraph cluster_core {
    label=<<i>Physics core</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    MP  [label="ModelParameters.jl"];
    MIC [label=<<b>Microclimate.jl</b>>, color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    SCI [label="SciML / OrdinaryDiffEq.jl"];
  }

  subgraph cluster_scale {
    label=<<i>Landscape</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    RAS [label="Rasters.jl"];
    RDS [label="RasterDataSources.jl"];
    GEO [label="Geomorphometry.jl"];
    MCP [label=<<b>MicroclimateMapper.jl</b>>, color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  subgraph cluster_eco {
    label=<<i>Ecology models</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    BBE [label="BiophysicalBehaviour.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    HEX [label="HeatExchange.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  subgraph cluster_bio {
    label=<<i>Biogeography models</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    SDM [label="SpeciesDistributionModels.jl"];
    ANM [label="AnimalMapper.jl", style="dashed,rounded,filled", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    PLM [label="PlantMapper.jl", style="dashed,rounded,filled", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  // Inter-cluster flow (anchored on the core nodes of each layer)
  SOL  -> MIC [ltail=cluster_forcing, lhead=cluster_core];
  FLU  -> MIC [ltail=cluster_forcing, lhead=cluster_core];
  MIC  -> MCP [ltail=cluster_core, lhead=cluster_scale];
  MIC  -> HEX [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  MCP  -> SDM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  BBE  -> ANM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  HEX  -> ANM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  HEX  -> PLM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
}
```

::: {.diagram-legend}
[BiophysicalEcology packages:]{.legend-title} &nbsp;&nbsp;
[ ]{.swatch .existing} existing &nbsp;&nbsp;
[ ]{.swatch .planned} planned &nbsp;&nbsp;
[ ]{.swatch .external} external
:::

:::
